slides.title: Why you wake up *foggy, not rested*.
slides.sub: Your core body temperature has to drop for deep sleep. About 3g of glycine before bed may help it fall faster.

slides.title: Sleep needs a temp drop
slides.body: Deep sleep kicks in when your *core temperature falls*. If it stays high, you toss and stay shallow.

slides.title: Glycine helps it drop
slides.body: Glycine widens blood vessels in your hands and feet, releasing heat so your *core cools faster*.

slides.title: About 3 grams
slides.body: Studies used *~3g about an hour* before bed. It's the glycine found in bone broth and collagen.

slides.title: Fall asleep, stay asleep
slides.body: People report falling asleep faster and waking *sharper in the morning* — less grogginess, more deep sleep.

slides.title: Pair with the basics
slides.body: Glycine isn't magic — pair it with a *cool, dark room* and a consistent bedtime.

slides.title: Wake up *actually rested*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your sleep and ties it to how sharp you feel the next day — so you can see what's really working.

caption:
Wake up foggy even after 8 hours? Your core body temperature might not be dropping enough. 😴

About 3g of glycine before bed may help your core cool faster — the signal for deep sleep. Pair it with a cool, dark room and a steady bedtime. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health tracks your sleep and ties it to how sharp you feel → link in bio.
youtube_title: The Amino Acid That May Deepen Your Sleep
